candidate_problems.text: Can 'controlled self-reference' be made into a precise mathematical consistency criterion for physical theories, distinguishing benign recursive closure from paradox-generating self-description, and does this criterion explain exactly where GR and QM each fail when extended to include observer, geometry, and measurement in one framework?

What concrete underlying structure could generate spacetime, observer-system distinctions, and definite outcomes as emergent features of a single pre-geometric relational dynamics, without presupposing a classical background or an external measurement interface?

By what mechanism do stable classical observers and quasi-classical spacetime emerge together across scales from a fundamentally reflexive quantum-gravitational substrate, and can this jointly explain locality, causality, and outcome definiteness rather than treating them as separate problems?
